description: Implements the full FlashAttention lineage (v1–v4) in pure PyTorch with algorithmic fidelity. Core algorithms: (1) IO-aware block-wise tiling partitioning Q/K/V into Br×d and Bc×d SRAM-sized tiles so the N×N attention matrix is never materialized; (2) online softmax with exact running statistics recurrence (m_new = max(m_old, max(S_tile)), ℓ_new rescaling with exp(m_old − m_new) correction factor) producing bitwise-identical output to standard attention; (3) recomputation-based backward pass trading FLOPs for memory by storing only O(N) log-sum-exp values and recomputing attention scores from Q,K; (4) fused kernel emulation. v2 refinements: Q-outer/KV-inner loop order swap keeping Q blocks resident in registers, Split-Q for warp-independent computation, delayed normalization reducing non-matmul FLOPs. v3 (Hopper): warp specialization with async TMA prefetch and ring shared memory barriers, ping-pong scheduling alternating GEMM and softmax warp groups to hide slow softmax behind fast matmul, FP8 low-precision with incoherent processing via random orthogonal pre-multiplication. v4 (Blackwell): software-emulated exp(x) via degree-3 FMA polynomial (Cody-Waite range reduction + Horner evaluation) bypassing saturated MUFU pipeline, conditional online softmax rescaling (threshold τ=8, eliminating ~90% of unnecessary rescales), 2-CTA MMA mode for halved shared memory traffic, TMEM scratchpad for backward pass intermediates. Also covers broader ecosystem: GQA/MQA (grouped/multi-query attention), Ring Attention for distributed near-infinite context, FlashDecoding for long-context inference, and roofline analysis utilities.

Implements Rotary Position Embeddings (RoPE) and the complete length extrapolation toolkit. Core algorithms: base RoPE with block-diagonal rotation matrices R_Θ,m = ⊕[cos(mθ_i), -sin(mθ_i); sin(mθ_i), cos(mθ_i)] where θ_i = b^{−2i/d} (b=10000), achieving relative-position dot product property (R_m q_m)ᵀ(R_n k_n) = q_mᵀ R_{n−m} k_n. Position Interpolation (PI): uniform rescaling of all position indices by factor s = L_target/L_train, mapping extended positions back into training range. NTK-aware scaling: adjusts RoPE base frequency b' = b · s^{d/(d−2)}, barely changing high-frequency dims (preserving local resolution) while heavily scaling low-frequency dims. NTK-by-parts: classifies each RoPE dimension by wavelength λ_i = 2π/θ_i relative to training length L — high freq (λ_i ≪ L, pure extrapolation), low freq (λ_i ≫ L, full interpolation θ_i/s), mid freq (linear ramp). YaRN (ICLR 2024): combines NTK-by-parts with attention temperature scaling t where sqrt(1/t) = 0.1·ln(s) + 1, restoring pre-extension entropy levels and preventing softmax 'sharpening' collapse. LongRoPE2: evolutionary search for optimal per-dimension rescaling factors guided by needle-driven perplexity. DPE: training-free detection of each dimension's effective length with targeted rescaling of only problematic dimensions, enabling 128K context with zero fine-tuning. ParallelComp: training-free parallel chunked attention with calibration achieving 4K→128K on single A100. Dynamic NTK: elastic sub-linear growth S(l') = max(1, γ·(l'/L)^κ) adapting scale factor to actual input length at inference. Attention entropy analysis: measures per-layer/per-head entropy to diagnose attention dilution and collapse as sequence length grows, implementing the entropy stabilization framework that unifies PI, NTK, YaRN and DPE.

Implements State Space Model architectures from S4 through Mamba-2 with mathematical fidelity to the continuous-time→discrete→recurrent→convolutional pipeline. Core algorithms: continuous-time SSM h'(t) = A·h(t) + B·u(t), y(t) = C·h(t) + D·u(t) with discretization via step size Δ yielding Ā = exp(ΔA), B̄ = (ΔA)^{−1}(exp(ΔA) − I)·ΔB. S4 (ICLR 2022): HiPPO theory parameterization of A providing mathematically optimal long-sequence compression, diagonal-plus-low-rank (DPLR) form enabling efficient convolution via Cauchy kernel. S4D: pure diagonal state matrices with simpler parameterization. H3 (ICLR 2023): dual SSMs with multiplicative gating — memory SSM (standard recurrence) and comparison SSM (shifted recurrence) enabling associative recall that pure S4 fails at. Mamba/S6 (Dec 2023): input-dependent selectivity with data-dependent B, C, Δ via learned linear projections, hardware-aware parallel scan achieving O(log N) parallel depth while maintaining O(N) total work via associative scan, unified Mamba block replacing SSM+MLP. Mamba-2/SSD (ICML 2024): the key theoretical advance — proves SSMs and attention are mathematically dual through semiseparable matrices where M_ji = C_j·A_{j−1}···A_{i+1}·B_i has rank ≤ N below diagonal. Structured Masked Attention (SMA) with multiplicative causal mask is equivalent to SSM with scalar-diagonal A. SSD layer unifies both perspectives with three computation modes: quadratic attention form (training), recurrent form (inference), and SSD block decomposition O(N²/B + BN) for tensor core training. Expanded state dimension 8× (N=16→256), multi-head support, tensor parallelism. Implements the full scan-based parallel associative scan for efficient GPU recurrence.

Implements linear recurrent and gated architectures as attention alternatives, covering RWKV, xLSTM, Griffin/Hawk, and RetNet. Core algorithms: RWKV (v4–v7): token-mixing with WKV operator O_i = σ(R_i)·Σe^{W_{i−j}+K_j}·V_j / Σe^{W_{i−j}+K_j} using learned per-channel exponential decay W (data-independent, enabling parallel training and pure recurrent inference with no KV cache), channel-mixing blocks, and RWKV-7's Generalized Delta Rule for state updates acting as implicit gradient descent on internal state (transcends TC⁰ complexity class for state tracking). xLSTM (NeurIPS 2024): exponential gating replacing sigmoid with exp(x) for decisive memory updates stabilized by normalizer state; dual memory — sLSTM (scalar memory) for state tracking and formal languages, mLSTM (matrix memory) with covariance update rule C_t = C_{t−1} + k_t⊗v_t storing key-value associations in d×d matrix, fully parallelizable. Griffin/Hawk (Google DeepMind 2024): Real-Gated Linear Recurrent Unit (RG-LRU) with diagonal recurrent weights constrained to 0 ≤ a ≤ 1 (stable), all-real arithmetic, no orthogonal polynomial initialization needed. Hawk: pure RG-LRU recurrence exceeding Mamba-3B with half the training tokens. Griffin: RG-LRU + local sliding-window attention, 6–7× fewer training tokens than Llama-2. RetNet: retention mechanism with dual parallel (chunkwise) and recurrent forms for both training and inference modes, combining multi-scale decay rates with group normalization.

Implements hybrid architectures that strategically combine attention with sub-quadratic sequence models, based on the key mechanistic insight of functional segregation (attention layers exclusively perform exact token-level retrieval while SSM layers handle long-range context compression). Core patterns: (1) Sliding window attention + SSM interleaving (attention on recent tokens for precise local interactions, SSM for global long-range context with O(N) complexity); (2) H3-style (Hungry Hungry Hippos) with dual SSMs gated by multiplicative interactions for associative recall; (3) GLA (Gated Linear Attention) with chunkwise parallel forms and hardware-efficient tiling that bridges linear attention and gated RNNs; (4) Mega-style moving average gated attention with exponential moving average components combining gated recurrence with chunked attention; (5) Unified sequence mixer abstraction supporting both attention and recurrent modes with configurable kernel fusion paths. Implements the three key hybrid design patterns from the literature: Jamba-style (Transformer + Mamba + MoE, 256K context, 3× throughput), Zamba-style (Mamba backbone + shared attention blocks, most sample-efficient), and NVIDIA 8B proven configuration (43% SSM + 7% Attention + 50% MLP achieving +2.65 pts above Transformer with 8× faster inference). Supports configurable attention-to-SSM ratios with the finding that even 7–10% attention layers dramatically improve hybrid performance — sparsifying to 15% of heads maintains near-perfect retrieval while preserving 84% of MMLU.

Comprehensive benchmarking and evaluation harness for comparing all implemented attention mechanisms. Measures and analyzes: (1) Throughput — tokens/sec, achieved TFLOPS vs theoretical peak (A100: 312 TFLOPS, H100: 989 TFLOPS, B200: 2.25 PFLOPS), compute utilization percentage; (2) Memory — peak HBM usage, memory bandwidth utilization via roofline model (plotting FLOP/byte vs arithmetic intensity with memory-bound vs compute-bound regions), SRAM/HBM traffic ratio; (3) Perplexity — standard benchmarks (WikiText-2, PG-19 for long-range), with degradation curves as context length increases beyond training length; (4) Length extrapolation quality — perplexity vs sequence length plots, passkey retrieval accuracy (Needle-in-a-Haystack), RULER and InfiniteBench comprehensive scores; (5) Attention entropy analysis — per-layer/per-head entropy distributions to diagnose attention dilution (entropy approaching theoretical maximum Θ(log n)) and embedding collapse toward low-frequency components; (6) Comparison tables across architectures (Standard Transformer, FlashAttention v1-v4, Mamba-2 pure, RWKV-7, Griffin, Mamba-2-Hybrid) with dimensions: complexity, inference memory, retrieval quality, throughput, perplexity; (7) Roofline plots visualizing arithmetic intensity vs FLOP/s for each implementation. Generates publication-quality matplotlib figures and LaTeX-compatible result tables.
